quiz1.missionText: Found someone great! Try sending a 'Like'

insight.title: How did you know a right swipe means 'Like'?
insight.subtitle: Right swipe is the world standard gesture for YES/approval
insight.swipeDesc: Tinder created the 'right for like, left for skip' swipe UI in 2012. Now it is the standard interaction adopted by matching apps worldwide.
insight.heartTitle: The heart button has the same effect
insight.heartDesc: Tapping the heart icon button at the bottom of the screen triggers the same 'Like' action. It is designed as an alternative UI to swiping.
insight.feedbackDesc: The animation of the card flying to the right intuitively conveys the result of the action. Visual feedback allows users to act without hesitation.

quiz2.missionText: Not quite your type... skip and see the next person

insight.title: How did you know a left swipe means 'Skip'?
insight.subtitle: Left swipe is the opposing gesture for NO/rejection
insight.swipeDesc: If right is YES, left is NO. This symmetrical design creates intuition. The same rule is used in any app with a card-based UI, not just matching apps.
insight.xTitle: The X button has the same effect
insight.xDesc: The X (cross) button at the bottom left means 'skip'. It leverages the universal icon language where 'X = reject/cancel'.
insight.gestureDesc: The consistent rule of 'right = OK, left = NG' allows users to start using a new app without explanation. A design philosophy that reduces learning cost to zero.

quiz3.missionText: I wonder if this person has other photos? Let's look at the second photo

insight.title: How did you know a tap would switch to the next photo?
insight.subtitle: Tapping to advance photos is a standard smartphone browsing operation
insight.tapTitle: Tap right = next photo, tap left = previous photo
insight.tapDesc: Tapping the right half of the profile card advances to the next photo; tapping the left half goes back. The same intuitive operation as Instagram Stories.
insight.indicatorTitle: The bars at the top are photo count indicators
insight.indicatorDesc: The short bars at the top of the card show the total number of photos and which one is currently displayed. The bright bar indicates the current position.
insight.multiPhotoDesc: In matching apps, having multiple photos viewed conveys personality and atmosphere better than just one. The number of photos is an important factor in selection.

quiz4.missionText: This is destiny!! Make a strong impression with a special 'Super Like'

insight.title: How did you know an upward swipe means 'Super Like'?
insight.subtitle: Upward swipe is the intuitive gesture for a special action
insight.superDesc: The upward swipe added by Tinder is a differentiating action that shows 'special interest'. It stands out as a third option beyond the right/left binary choice.
insight.starTitle: The star button also triggers Super Like
insight.starDesc: Tapping the star button at the bottom of the screen triggers the same Super Like. This allows reliable operation even in situations where swiping is difficult.
insight.specialDesc: Super Like notifies the other person more strongly than a regular like, said to increase match rate. A design that leverages the psychology that value increases the more limited something is.
